questions.question: Coogee Beach, which was seen in news due to discovery of mysterious debris, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: On October 15, 2024, mysterious black, ball-shaped debris, likely tar balls, were discovered along Coogee Beach, Australia. Tar balls formed as a result of oil spills, mixing oil with seawater, sand, and debris into a hardened substance. Their size ranged from small pebbles to fist-sized, with surfaces that varied from smooth to rough. They posed environmental risks to marine life and human health due to toxic components like polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs).

questions.question: What is the name of the exercise recently conducted by the Sudarshan Chakra Corps at Jhansi?
questions.explanation: The ‘Exercise SWAVLAMBAN SHAKTI’ is conducted by the Sudarshan Chakra Corps at Babina Field Firing Ranges near Jhansi. This exercise focuses on integrating niche and emerging technologies into the Indian Army’s offensive strategies. It involves approximately 1,800 personnel and showcases over 50 new technology equipment (NTEs) from more than 40 industry partners, including advanced drones and robotic systems. The aim is to enhance combat readiness and foster collaboration between the Indian Army and the defence industry, boosting indigenous defence manufacturing capabilities.

questions.question: Which state government has announced free dialysis for chronic kidney patients in government hospitals?
questions.explanation: Haryana government has announced free dialysis services for chronic kidney patients in all government hospitals. This initiative aims to reduce the financial burden on patients, who usually spend ₹20,000 to ₹25,000 per month on dialysis. The Haryana government will cover these expenses, showing its commitment to supporting marginalized communities and enhancing healthcare.

questions.question: What is the theme for World Osteoporosis Day 2024?

options.label: B
options.text: Step Up for Bone Health-Build Better Bones

questions.explanation: World Osteoporosis Day is observed on October 20 each year to raise awareness about osteoporosis and metabolic bone diseases. Organized by the International Osteoporosis Foundation (IOF), the campaign engages communities in over 90 countries with local initiatives. Osteoporosis weakens bones, making them fragile and prone to fractures, especially among postmenopausal women and the elderly. The day began in the UK in 1996, with the IOF taking over in 1997. The 2024 theme, “Say No to Fragile Bones,” emphasizes the need for proactive measures to protect bone health and reduce fracture risks.

questions.question: Sri Singeeswarar Temple, which was seen in the news, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Copper plate inscriptions from the 16th century were found at Sri Singeeswarar temple in Tiruvallur, Tamil Nadu. The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and located in Mappedu Village. It was originally built by Aditya Karikalan II in 976 AD, father of Chola emperor Rajaraja Cholan. Krishnadevaraya’s viceroy, Dalavai Ariyanadha Mudaliar, added the Rajagopuram, compound wall, and 16-pillar Mandap in 1501. The temple features Dravidian architecture with a 5-tier Rajagopuram. The main deity, Singeeswarar, is worshipped in the form of a large Shiva Lingam.

questions.question: Which state government recently launched Mission Basundhara 3.0 to provide land rights to indigenous people?
questions.explanation: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma launched Mission Basundhara 3.0 to grant land rights to indigenous people and digitize land services. The initiative aims to improve transparency and efficiency in land-related processes. It simplifies the process of granting land rights by implementing new rules and recognizing community-specific exceptions. Mission Basundhara 3.0 also focuses on digitizing land services and publishing land maps for public benefit.

questions.question: Which city in India recently witnessed bioluminescent waves?
questions.explanation: Chennai recently witnessed bioluminescent waves, a natural phenomenon caused by marine organisms like dinoflagellates. These microscopic plankton emit light at night, visible on the ocean surface. Bioluminescence is common among many marine animals such as jellyfish, fish, and algae, used for evading predators, attracting prey, or mating. It occurs when the enzyme luciferase reacts with luciferin in the presence of oxygen. This phenomenon is often visible in warm weather and low light. Other beaches in India like Havelock Island, Mattu Beach, and Bangaram Island have also experienced it.

questions.question: Which country recently hosted the Exercise Kakadu?
questions.explanation: The Quad countries—India, Australia, Japan, and the U.S.—conducted back-to-back naval exercises, focusing on anti-submarine warfare. ‘Exercise Malabar’ took place off the coast of Visakhapatnam from October 8 to 18, 2024. It was termed the most comprehensive edition yet, with complex operational scenarios. The earlier ‘Exercise Kakadu,’ hosted by Australia, involved nearly 3,000 personnel from 30 nations. Ships from 10 countries and aircraft from five nations participated, highlighting Australia’s commitment to regional maritime security. The Indian Navy contributed a P-8I Maritime Patrol Aircraft for the exercise.

questions.question: Which South Korean river project is being used as a model for the rejuvenation of the Musi River in Telangana?
questions.explanation: The Telangana government aims to develop Musi riverbanks for commercial use, inspired by South Korea’s Great Hangang (Han River) project. The Han River is South Korea’s fourth-longest river, flowing through Seoul. It originates from the Namhan and Bukhan rivers in eastern South Korea. The river flows through Seoul and empties into the Yellow Sea. Telangana’s plan was to attract private firms to develop riverfronts similar to the Han River model.

questions.question: Which organization has recently launched the Amazon Future Engineer Program in 50 Eklavya Model Residential Schools (EMRS)?

options.label: A
options.text: National Education Society for Tribal Students (NESTS)

answer.label: A
answer.text: National Education Society for Tribal Students (NESTS)

questions.explanation: The National Education Society for Tribal Students (NESTS) and Amazon launched the third phase of the Amazon Future Engineer Program in 50 Eklavya Model Residential Schools (EMRS). The program was implemented in schools across Andhra Pradesh, Gujarat, Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, Telangana, and Tripura. It focused on training in emerging technologies like blockchain, artificial intelligence, block programming, and coding. Students in classes 6-9 learned computer science fundamentals, while Class 10 students engaged in project-based virtual sessions. The objective was to prepare tribal students for STEM careers through modern technological literacy.
